body: # Inland water slab [DOCS](https://clima.github.io/ClimaLand.jl/previews/PR1672/standalone/pages/inland_waters/slab_lake/) This PR adds a fixed-depth slab lake representation for inland water grid points (lakes, rivers, reservoirs). A prognostic `SlabLakeModel` (in its own `InlandWater` module) evolves lake internal energy (`U_lake`) at the surface while the underlying soil column acts as a thermal sediment substrate. ## Key features - `SlabLakeModel` as a standalone component with `SlabLakeParameters` (configurable depth, conductance, albedo liquid/ice, emissivity, roughness lengths) - Prognostic lake internal energy with phase change (liquid fraction `q_l` determines ice/liquid state) - Lake–sediment heat exchange via a single tunable conductance parameter (series-resistance model with `Δz_top` read from the soil domain at runtime) - Fixed-depth assumption: net surface water flux (P − E) balanced by signed runoff residual - Energy conservation: surface energy balance + sediment heat flux + runoff enthalpy = dU_lake/dt - Continuous blending of soil and lake fluxes via `lake_fraction` mask - Lake diagnostics: `lkie` (lake internal energy), `tlake` (temperature), `qlake` (liquid fraction) - Inland water mask derived from [IMERG land-sea mask](https://gpm.nasa.gov/data/directory/imerg-land-sea-mask-netcdf) (water fraction > 80%, latitude ±60°), located in `InlandWater.inland_water_mask()` ## What changed at lake grid points - Surface fluxes drive the slab lake energy equation, blended with soil via lake fraction - Soil receives heat only through the lake–sediment interface (no direct atmospheric forcing at lake points) - Soil sublimation source masked at lake points - LAI, SAI, and RAI zeroed at lake points (no canopy over lakes) - Ground albedo and LW upwelling area-weighted across soil, snow, and lake fractions - Surface albedo and specific humidity vary with ice fraction; roughness uses lake values ## Architecture - Lake component uses dispatch (`Nothing` vs `SlabLakeModel`) throughout — no runtime `if !isnothing` checks in per-step code - `soil_boundary_fluxes!` dispatches on `Val(prognostic_land_components)` for lake-aware tuples - Radiation dispatch (`ground_albedo_PAR/NIR`, `_ground_lw_upwelling`, `_update_lake_radiative_fluxes!`) uses separate methods for with-lake and without-lake component tuples - Sediment heat flux computation lives in `src/integrated/soil_lake_model.jl`, following the same pattern as `update_soil_snow_ground_heat_flux!` in `soil_snow_model.jl` - `lake_fraction` is a static cache field (set once at initialization from `inland_water_mask`) needed by canopy ground albedo dispatch methods ## Follow-up work - Ice-dependent roughness lengths (currently constant for liquid and frozen states) - Standalone lake mode (currently lake only runs in integrated mode with a soil model) Note: adjusting the mask water fraction threshold (currently using above 80% water) might reduce RMSE further a bit. Not tested yet. Note2, for discussion: mask is currently binary. Do we want to ensure the code will work in case in the future we use a non-binary lake fraction f?
title: inland waters slab

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Reverts this change from #1591. Requires also reverting bucket changes from https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/commit/288987875b84413fc01e5de6a8826f11abab9261#diff-a2880400c2145db122968b23c23a980c061601611b3bfe9e2cce55c10f147c73

Tested in this ClimaCoupler PR and works fine https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/pull/1865

## To-do
- [x] add a method of `turbulent_fluxes!` which does nothing in `CoupledAtmosphere` case
- [x] add a function `helper_update_surface_humidity_function` so we can remap `W` and `σS` onto the boundary space and call it on those in the coupler

title: don't compute fluxes in coupled case
